title: Fixing Incorrect Type Inference for a Generic Class with Method Overloading

statement.markdown: Write a generic class `Container<T>` that has a method `add(item: T)` and another method `add(items: T[]): void`. The `add(items: T[]): void` method should correctly infer the type of the items array as `T[]` but the current implementation is incorrectly inferring it as `any[]`. Fix the bug to ensure correct type inference.
statement.inputFormat: Input format is a JSON object with a single property `items` of type `T[]`.
statement.outputFormat: Output format is a JSON object with a single property `result` of type `T[]`.
statement.notes: The class should support method overloading and correct type inference for the `items` array.

constraints.description: The number of items to add to the container.

examples.explanation: Add three numbers to the container.
Add three strings to the container.

hints.text: Check the type parameters of the generic class.
Verify the method overloading is correctly implemented.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: The key to solving this problem is to correctly implement method overloading and ensure the type parameters of the generic class are correctly used.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible brute force approach is to create a separate method for adding a single item and another method for adding multiple items.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution is to use method overloading and ensure the type parameters are correctly used.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use method overloading and correctly infer the type of the items array.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is that the solution correctly infers the type of the items array and supports method overloading.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: 1. Create a generic class Container<T> with type parameters T. 2. Implement the add(item: T) method. 3. Implement the add(items: T[]): void method using method overloading.
commonMistakes: Incorrectly inferring the type of the items array
